pattern: The person at pos never affects their own visibility count, so their choice is a free factor of two; everyone else independently rolls a coin toward being counted, and Vandermonde's convolution collapses the two-sided combinatorics into one binomial coefficient.
hints: The person standing at pos always has a free, unconstrained choice between the two directions -- it contributes a flat factor of 2 to every valid assignment, independent of everything else.

Everyone to the left is counted only if they pick the direction that faces the pos person, and everyone to the right only if they pick the other direction -- so, ignoring pos, you're really just choosing how many of the remaining n - 1 people are "visible" in total.

Summing, over every way to split k visible people between the left group and the right group, the product of two binomial coefficients is exactly Vandermonde's identity -- it collapses to choosing k visible people out of all n - 1 non-pos people at once.

edge_inputs.description: single person, no one else to see
pos at the very start of the line
pos at the very end of the line
pos in the middle with an even split of neighbors
k equal to zero with people on both sides
k equal to the maximum possible visible count
pos adjacent to one end, asymmetric left/right counts
large n with pos and k at the extremes

stress.description: large n with random pos and k within range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    pos = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    k = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    return [n, pos, k]
